overview: Rome, the capital city of Italy, is a vibrant metropolis steeped in history and culture. Located in the central-western part of the Italian Peninsula, it serves as an important political, economic, and cultural hub in Europe. With a population of approximately 2.3 million, Rome is renowned for its ancient landmarks, stunning architecture, and rich artistic heritage, making it a vital destination for travelers and scholars alike.
history: Founded in 753 BC, Rome has long been a center of power, culture, and religion. It was the heart of the Roman Empire, which laid the foundations for modern Western civilization.
economy: Rome's economy is diverse, with key industries including tourism, film, fashion, and technology. The city is home to numerous multinational corporations and serves as a significant center for commerce and trade in Europe.
tourism: Rome attracts millions of tourists each year, drawn by its iconic attractions such as the Colosseum, Vatican City, and the Pantheon. Visitors often indulge in the city’s rich culinary scene, explore its historic neighborhoods, and enjoy its vibrant street life.

best_restaurants.name: La Pergola
best_restaurants.description: A Michelin-starred restaurant offering exquisite Italian cuisine with stunning views of the city.

best_restaurants.name: Trattoria Da Enzo
best_restaurants.description: A cozy eatery known for its authentic Roman dishes and warm atmosphere.

best_restaurants.name: Pizzeria La Montecarlo
best_restaurants.description: Famous for its delicious thin-crust pizzas and local vibe.

best_restaurants.name: Ristorante Aroma
best_restaurants.description: Offers a fine dining experience with breathtaking views of the Colosseum.

best_restaurants.name: Gelateria Giolitti
best_restaurants.description: Renowned for its traditional Italian gelato, a must-try for visitors.

weather_summary: Rome experiences mild, rainy winters and hot, dry summers with occasional heat waves.

faqs.question: What time zone is Rome in?
faqs.answer: Rome is in the Europe/Rome time zone.

faqs.question: Does Rome observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rome observes daylight saving time, typically from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October.

faqs.question: What is the population of Rome?
faqs.answer: The population of Rome is approximately 2,318,895.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Rome?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Rome typically occurs between 6:30 AM and 7:00 AM, depending on the season.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Rome?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Rome is during the spring (April to June) and fall (September to October) when the weather is pleasant.
